author: mark
text:
Deferred-squash review mode: push branch, auto-open gate

Implements delivery.mode=deferred (SPEC §7.2). When set, bl review pushes work/<id> to the code remote, opens a forge-gate child task, links the parent gates->child, and flips it to review — without squashing into target_branch or setting delivered_in. Deferred mode requires an explicit target_branch (SPEC §5); an implicit checkout target is rejected before any mutation.

The auto-gate reuses the existing close-blocker: an old bl that never heard of deferred mode still refuses bl close on a gated parent, so the worktree cannot be torn down mid-review. No new lifecycle state, no old-client code (SPEC §3/§4, conformance §14.5).

Also centralizes unique-id minting in balls::task_id (the retry loop had been duplicated; cmd_create and sync_report now route through the one home) and extracts commit_msg::subject_with_tag so the PR-title hint and the squash subject share one tagged-title builder.

Out of scope, filed as follow-ups under bl-00ca: the deferred-mode reject path (bl-3cc2, SPEC §7.3) and advisory min_bl_version (bl-b7d3, SPEC §5). Tests: tests/forge_deferred.rs covers conformance §14.4-6 plus the explicit-target rule; full suite green, 100% coverage.
